You are the SIMILARITY AUDITOR in a clean-room process (see /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md).
You MAY read both sides — that is your function. A clean-room agent implemented a crate purely from
black-box oracles; you now compare it against the GPL Haskell originals to catch expression that could
not plausibly have come from observation, including inadvertent reproduction from model training memory.

Apply abstraction-filtration-comparison discipline. NOT violations (filter these out): identical output
strings (warning texts, DOT/HTML/JSON syntax, templates) — these are compatibility content the clean
side copied from OBSERVED OUTPUT; identical behavior/algorithms at the idea level; API names given in
the provided interface docs; structure forced by the data model or the output format.
VIOLATIONS to flag: internal helper decomposition mirroring the Haskell where alternatives exist;
internal (non-API, non-output) names matching Haskell internals; comments echoing Haskell comments;
algorithmic EXPRESSION matching where the observed behavior admits materially different implementations;
any content that appears in the Haskell source but NOT in any observable output.

Write a detailed audit to the specified AUDIT.md (per-module notes, each finding with clean-side
file:line, Haskell file:line, and why it survives filtration), and return ONLY the small summary object.
For each finding include a REDO INSTRUCTION phrased behaviorally (what to reimplement differently),
never a code patch.
CLEAN SIDE: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/graph-clean/src/ (model.rs, dot.rs, term.rs, abbrev.rs, lib.rs).
HASKELL ORIGINALS: /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/{Dot.hs,GraphRepr.hs,Graph/Abbreviation.hs,Graph/Simplification.hs} — locate exact paths with git ls-files in the submodule (Abbreviation/Simplification may live under Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/).
Secondary: /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/tamarin-server/src/graph/*.rs and handlers/dot.rs.
Write audit to /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/AUDIT.md

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).

69	-- | The style for nodes of the intruder.
70	data BoringNodeStyle = FullBoringNodes | CompactBoringNodes
71	    deriving( Eq, Ord, Show )
72	
73	-- | Options for dot generation.
74	data DotOptions = DotOptions
75	  { _doNodeStyle :: BoringNodeStyle -- ^ The style for nodes of the intruder.
76	  , _doAbbrevColor :: D.Color
77	  }
78	    deriving ( Eq, Ord, Show )
79	
80	-- | The default dot options.
81	defaultDotOptions :: DotOptions
82	defaultDotOptions = DotOptions CompactBoringNodes black
83	  where
84	    black = D.RGB 0 0 0
85	
86	$(mkLabels [''DotOptions])
87	
88	type NodeColorMap = M.Map (RuleInfo ProtoRuleACInstInfo IntrRuleACInfo) (RGB Rational)
89	type SeDot = ReaderT (Graph, NodeColorMap, DotOptions) (StateT DotState D.Dot)
90	
91	-- | State to track the dot NodeId (different from a System NodeId) of created dot components.
92	data DotState = DotState {
93	    _dsNodes   :: M.Map NodeId   D.NodeId
94	  , _dsPrems   :: M.Map NodePrem D.NodeId
95	  , _dsConcs   :: M.Map NodeConc D.NodeId
96	  -- a.d. TODO this is unused. Check if I droppped it somewhere, else delete.
97	  , _dsSingles :: M.Map (NodeConc, NodePrem) D.NodeId
98	  }
99	
100	$(mkLabels [''DotState])
101	
102	-- | Wrap a computation that generates dot components (SeDot) in order cache that component in the DotState and be able to retrieve its NodeId.
103	cacheState :: Ord k
104	           => (DotState :-> M.Map k D.NodeId) -- ^ Accessor to map storing this type of actions.
105	           -> k                               -- ^ Action index.
106	           -> SeDot D.NodeId
107	           -> SeDot ()
108	cacheState stateAccessor k dot = do
109	    i <- dot
110	    modM stateAccessor (M.insert k i)
111	
112	-- | Retrieve a NodeId from a cached dot component.
113	getState :: Ord k
114	         => (DotState :-> M.Map k D.NodeId)
115	         -> k
116	         -> String
117	         -> SeDot D.NodeId
118	getState stateAccessor k msg = do
119	    stateMap <- getM stateAccessor
120	    let mi = stateMap M.!? k
121	    case mi of
122	      Nothing -> error msg
123	      Just i -> return i
124	
125	-- | Lift a 'D.Dot' action.
126	liftDot :: D.Dot a -> SeDot a
127	liftDot = lift . lift
128	
129	-- | Set default attributes for nodes and edges.
130	setDefaultAttributes :: D.Dot ()
131	setDefaultAttributes = do
132	  D.attribute ("nodesep","0.3")
133	  D.attribute ("ranksep","0.3")
134	  D.nodeAttributes [("fontsize","8"),("fontname","Helvetica"),("width","0.3"),("height","0.2")]
135	  D.edgeAttributes [("fontsize","8"),("fontname","Helvetica")]
136	
137	
138	-- | Set default attributes for nodes and edges if the graph contains clusters.
139	-- Some additional options have been added for better rendering
140	setDefaultAttributesIfCluster :: D.Dot ()
141	setDefaultAttributesIfCluster = do
142	  D.attribute ("nodesep", "0.8") -- Slight increase to space out the graph
143	  D.attribute ("ranksep", "0.8")
144	  D.attribute ("sep", "4")
145	  D.attribute ("splines", "true")
146	  D.attribute ("overlap", "false") -- Prevent nodes from overlapping
147	  D.attribute ("pack", "true") -- Enable packing of clusters to ensure they are tightly grouped.
148	  D.attribute ("packmode", "cluster") -- Define packing mode specifically for clusters to organize them more efficiently.
149	  D.attribute ("concentrate", "true") -- Combine parallel edges .
150	  D.attribute ("compound", "true")
151	  D.attribute ("remincross", "true") -- Minimize edge crossings to improve readability.
152	  D.attribute ("mclimit", "10")
153	  D.attribute ("nslimit", "20")
154	  D.attribute ("nslimit1", "20")
155	  D.attribute ("ordering", "out")
156	  D.attribute ("rankdir", "TB") -- Set the direction of ranks from top to bottom for a hierarchical layout.
157	  D.attribute ("showboxes", "false")
158	  D.attribute ("clusterrank", "local") -- Rank clusters locally to ensure internal cluster organization
159	
160	  D.nodeAttributes [("fontsize", "8"), ("fontname", "Helvetica"), ("width", "0.3"), ("height", "0.2"), ("margin", "0.05,0.05"), ("shape", "ellipse")] -- Define default attributes for nodes with specific font and size settings.
161	  D.edgeAttributes [("fontsize", "8"), ("fontname", "Helvetica"), ("penwidth", "1.5"), ("arrowsize", "0.5"), ("color", "black"), ("style", "solid"), ("weight", "8")] -- Define default attributes for edges with specific font, size, and style settings.
162	
163	
164	
165	-- | This function creates a cluster for a specific role with a list of nodes.
166	-- It takes an role name and a 'SeDot' action, then executing the given SeDot action within
167	--a temporary state, updating the current state, and adding the resulting subgraph elements to
168	--the global state.
169	
170	-- Note: To be compatible with the 'dotNodeCompact' function which returns a 'SeDot' action,
171	-- we needed a function that accepts and returns a 'SeDot' action. This is why we didn't use
172	-- the 'cluster' function from 'Text.Dot'. The only feasible approach was either this or
173	-- fragmenting the 'dotNodeCompact' function. Thus, we extracted what we needed and removed
174	-- the unnecessary parts.
175	roleCluster :: String -> SeDot a -> SeDot ()
176	roleCluster roleName dot = do
177	  uq <- liftDot D.nextId
178	  let cid = D.createClusterNodeId roleName
179	  env <- ask
180	  currentState <- State.get
181	  let clusterState = D.DotGenState { D._dgsId = uq, D._dgsElements = [] }
182	  ((_, newState), finalDotState) <- lift . lift . lift $ runStateT (runStateT (runReaderT dot env) currentState) clusterState
183	  State.put newState
184	  _ <- liftDot $ D.setId $ D._dgsId finalDotState
185	  liftDot $ D.addElements [D.createSubGraph (Just cid) (D._dgsElements finalDotState)]
186	
187	
188	-- | Compute a color map for nodes labelled with a proof rule info of one of
189	-- the given rules.
190	nodeColorMap :: [RuleACInst] -> NodeColorMap
191	nodeColorMap rules =
192	    M.fromList $
193	      [ (get rInfo ru, getColorForRule (ruleAttributes ru) gIdx mIdx)
194	      | (gIdx, grp) <- groups, (mIdx, ru) <- zip [0..] grp ]
195	  where
196	    groupIdx ru
197	      | isDestrRule ru                   = 0
198	      | isConstrRule ru                  = 2
199	      | isFreshRule ru || isISendRule ru = 3
200	      | otherwise                        = 1
201	
202	    -- groups of rules labeled with their index in the group
203	    groups = [ (gIdx, [ ru | ru <- rules, gIdx == groupIdx ru])
204	             | gIdx <- [0..3]
205	             ]
206	
207	    -- color for each member of a group
208	    colors = M.fromList $ lightColorGroups intruderHue (map (length . snd) groups)
209	    getColor idx = fromMaybe (HSV 0 1 1) $ M.lookup idx colors
210	
211	    -- Function to get color for a given rule
212	    getColorForRule attrs gIdx mIdx = fromMaybe defaultColor (ruleColor attrs)
213	        where
214	            defaultColor =  hsvToRGB $ getColor (gIdx, mIdx)
215	
216	    -- The hue of the intruder rules
217	    intruderHue :: Rational
218	    intruderHue = 18 % 360
219	
220	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
221	-- Record based dotting
222	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
223	
224	-- | Render an LNFact using the abbreviations given by the graph.
225	renderLNFact :: Document d => LNFact -> SeDot d
226	renderLNFact fact = do
227	  (graph, _, _) <- ask
228	  let abbreviate = get ((L..) goAbbreviate gOptions) graph
229	      abbrevs = get gAbbreviations graph
230	      replacedFact = applyAbbreviationsFact (lookupAbbreviation abbrevs) fact
231	  if abbreviate
232	    then return $ prettyLNFact replacedFact
233	    else return $ prettyLNFact fact
234	
235	-- | Dot a node in record based (compact) format.
236	dotNodeCompact :: Node -> Maybe String -> SeDot ()
237	dotNodeCompact node manualNodeColor = do
238	  let v = get nNodeId node
239	  (graph, colorMap, dotOptions) <- ask
240	  case get nNodeType node of
241	    SystemNode ru -> cacheState dsNodes v $ do
242	      let outgoingEdge = hasOutgoingEdge graph v
243	      let role = fromMaybe "Undefined" (getNodeRole node)
244	
245	      let rInfoVal = get rInfo ru
246	
247	      -- TODO this is pretty ugly. Shorten using standard functions
248	      let ruleColor' = case rInfoVal of
249	                        ProtoInfo protoRule ->
250	                          case ruleColor (_praciAttributes protoRule) of
251	                            Just rgb -> Just (rgbToHex rgb)
252	                            _ -> Nothing
253	                        _ -> Nothing
254	
255	      let color = M.lookup rInfoVal colorMap
256	          nodeColor = fromMaybe (maybe "white" rgbToHex color) (ruleColor' <|> manualNodeColor)
257	          attrs = [("fillcolor", nodeColor), ("style", "filled")
258	                  , ("fontcolor", if colorUsesWhiteFont color then "white" else "black")
259	                  , ("role", role)]
260	
261	      ids <- mkNode v ru attrs outgoingEdge dotOptions
262	      let prems = [ ((v, i), nid) | (Just (Left i),  nid) <- ids ]
263	          concs = [ ((v, i), nid) | (Just (Right i), nid) <- ids ]
264	      modM dsPrems $ M.union $ M.fromList prems
265	      modM dsConcs $ M.union $ M.fromList concs
266	      return $ fromJust $ lookup Nothing ids
267	    UnsolvedActionNode facts -> cacheState dsNodes v $ do
268	      lblPre <- (fsep <$> punctuate comma <$> mapM renderLNFact facts)
269	      let lbl = lblPre <-> opAction <-> text (show v)
270	      let attrs | any isKUFact facts = [("color","gray")]
271	                | otherwise          = [("color","darkblue")]
272	      mkSimpleNode (render lbl) attrs
273	    LastActionAtom -> cacheState dsNodes v $ mkSimpleNode (show v) []
274	    MissingNode (Left conc) -> cacheState dsConcs (v, conc) $ dotConcC (v, conc)
275	    MissingNode (Right prem) -> cacheState dsPrems (v, prem) $ dotPremC (v, prem)
276	  where
277	    hasOutgoingEdge graph v =
278	      let repr = get gRepr graph
279	      in or [ v == v' | SystemEdge ((v', _), _) <- get grEdges repr ]
280	    missingNode shape label = liftDot $ D.node $ [("label", render label),("shape",shape)]
281	    dotPremC prem = missingNode "invtrapezium" $ prettyNodePrem prem
282	    dotConcC conc = missingNode "trapezium" $ prettyNodeConc conc
283	
284	    --True if there's a colour, and it's 'darker' than 0.5 in apparent luminosity
285	    --This assumes a linear colourspace, which is what graphviz seems to use
286	    colorUsesWhiteFont (Just (RGB r g b)) = (0.2126*r + 0.7152*g + 0.0722*b) < 0.5
287	    colorUsesWhiteFont _                  = False
288	
289	    mkSimpleNode lbl attrs =
290	        liftDot $ D.node $ [("label", lbl),("shape","ellipse")] ++ attrs
291	
292	    mkNode  :: NodeId -> RuleACInst -> [(String, String)] -> Bool -> DotOptions
293	      -> SeDot [(Maybe (Either PremIdx ConcIdx), D.NodeId)]
294	    mkNode v ru attrs outgoingEdge dotOptions
295	      -- single node, share node-id for all premises and conclusions
296	      | get doNodeStyle dotOptions == CompactBoringNodes &&
297	        (isIntruderRule ru || isFreshRule ru) = do
298	            ps <- psM
299	            as <- asM
300	            cs <- csM
301	            let lbl | outgoingEdge = show v ++ " : " ++ showDotRuleCaseName ru
302	                    | otherwise       = concatMap snd as
303	            nid <- mkSimpleNode lbl []
304	            return [ (key, nid) | (key, _) <- ps ++ as ++ cs ]
305	      -- full record syntax
306	      | otherwise = do
307	            ps <- psM
308	            as <- asM
309	            cs <- csM
310	            fmap snd $ liftDot $ (`D.record` attrs) $
311	              D.vcat $ map D.hcat $ map (map (uncurry D.portField)) $
312	              filter (not . null) [ps, as, cs]
313	      where
314	        psM = do
315	          row <- mapM (\(i, p) -> do
316	            lbl <- renderLNFact p
317	            return (Just (Left i), lbl)
318	            ) $ enumPrems ru
319	          return $ renderRow row
320	        asM = do
321	          ruleLabel <- ruleLabelM
322	          return $ renderRow [ (Nothing, ruleLabel ) ]
323	        csM = do
324	          row <- mapM (\(i, c) -> do
325	            lbl <- renderLNFact c
326	            return (Just (Right i), lbl)
327	            ) $ enumConcs ru
328	          return $ renderRow row
329	
330	        ruleLabelM = do
331	          showAutoSource <- asks (get ((L..) goShowAutoSource gOptions) . fst3)
332	          lbl <- if showAutoSource
333	                      then mapM renderLNFact $ filter isAutoSource $ filter isNotDiffAnnotation $ get rActs ru
334	                      else mapM renderLNFact $ filter isNotDiffAnnotation $ get rActs ru
335	          return $
336	            prettyNodeId v <-> colon
337	            <-> text (showDotRuleCaseName ru)
338	            <> (if null lbl then mempty else brackets (vcat $ punctuate comma lbl))
339	
340	
341	        isNotDiffAnnotation fa = (fa /= (Fact (ProtoFact Linear ("Diff" ++ getRuleNameDiff ru) 0) S.empty []))
342	
343	        -- check if a fact is from auto-source
344	        isAutoSource ::  LNFact -> Bool
345	        isAutoSource (Fact tag _ _) =not $ hasAutoLabel (showFactTag $ tag)
346	
347	        -- check if a fact has the label of auto-source
348	        hasAutoLabel :: String -> Bool
349	        hasAutoLabel f
350	            | "AUTO_IN_TERM_" `isPrefixOf` f  = True
351	            | "AUTO_IN_FACT_" `isPrefixOf` f  = True
352	            | "AUTO_OUT_TERM_" `isPrefixOf` f = True
353	            | "AUTO_OUT_FACT_" `isPrefixOf` f = True
354	            | otherwise = False
355	
356	
357	        renderRow annDocs =
358	          zipWith (\(ann, _) lbl -> (ann, lbl)) annDocs $
359	            -- magic factor 1.3 compensates for space gained due to
360	            -- non-propertional font
361	            renderBalanced 100 (max 30 . round . (* 1.3)) (map snd annDocs)
362	
363	        renderBalanced :: Double           -- ^ Total available width
364	                       -> (Double -> Int)  -- ^ Convert available space to actual line-width.
365	                       -> [Doc]            -- ^ Initial documents
366	                       -> [String]         -- ^ Rendered documents
367	        renderBalanced _          _    []   = []
368	        renderBalanced totalWidth conv docs =
369	            zipWith (\w d -> widthRender (conv (ratio * w)) d) usedWidths docs
370	          where
371	            oneLineRender  = renderStyle (defaultStyle { mode = OneLineMode })
372	            widthRender w  = scaleIndent . renderStyle (defaultStyle { lineLength = w })
373	            usedWidths     = map (fromIntegral . length . oneLineRender) docs
374	            ratio          = totalWidth / sum usedWidths
375	            scaleIndent line = case span isSpace line of
376	              (spaces, rest) ->
377	                  -- spaces are not wide-enough by default => scale them up
378	                  let n = (1.5::Double) * fromIntegral (length spaces)
379	                  in  replicate (round n) ' ' ++ rest

384	dotEdge edge =
385	  case edge of
386	    SystemEdge (src, tgt) -> do
387	      (graph, _, _) <- ask
388	      let check p = maybe False p (resolveNodePremFact tgt graph) ||
389	                    maybe False p (resolveNodeConcFact src graph)
390	          attrs | check isProtoFact =
391	                    [("style","bold"),("weight","10.0")] ++
392	                    (guard (check isPersistentFact) >> [("color","gray50")])
393	                | check isKFact     = [("color","orangered2")]
394	                | otherwise         = [("color","gray30")]
395	      dotGenEdge attrs src tgt
396	    UnsolvedChain (src, tgt) ->
397	      dotGenEdge [("style","dotted"),("color","green")] src tgt
398	    LessEdge _ -> error "LessEdges are handled by dotLessEdge"
399	  where
400	    dotGenEdge style src tgt = do
401	      srcId <- getState dsConcs src ("Source node of edge not found: " ++ show src)
402	      tgtId <- getState dsPrems tgt ("Target node of edge not found: " ++ show tgt)
403	      liftDot $ D.edge srcId tgtId style

412	-- | Dot a legend listing all abbreviations by adding a sink node with a suitable HTML table label.
413	generateLegend :: SeDot ()
414	generateLegend = do
415	  (graph, _, dotOptions) <- ask
416	  let abbrevs = get gAbbreviations graph
417	  -- Skip generating anything if no abbreviations exist.
418	  unless (null abbrevs) $ do
419	    nLegend <- liftDot $ D.scope (do
420	      D.attribute ("rank", "sink")
421	      let sortedAbbrevs = topoSortAbbrevs $ zip [0..] $
422	            sortOn (Data.Ord.Down . render . Sys.prettyLNTerm . fst) $ M.elems abbrevs
423	          labelColor = get doAbbrevColor dotOptions
424	          htmlLabel = D.htmlLabel $ abbrevLabel sortedAbbrevs labelColor
425	      D.node [("shape", "plain"), htmlLabel])
426	    -- We add invisible edges from all sink nodes of the graph to the legend node to place it somewhere in the middle of the bottom row.
427	    -- We only add edges from the sink nodes because edges from earlier nodes will be routed avoid later nodes (even if they are invisible) and create constraints that lead to excessive whitespace on the edges of the graph.
428	    let sinks = getGraphSinks graph
429	    dotIds <- getM dsNodes
430	    mapM_ (\nsink ->
431	      case M.lookup (get nNodeId nsink) dotIds of
432	      Nothing -> pure ()
433	      Just nid -> liftDot $ D.edge nid nLegend [("style", "invis")]) sinks
434	  where
435	    -- | Render all abbreviations as a table using graphviz' HTML notation.
436	    abbrevLabel sortedAbbrevs labelColor =
437	      let tableAttributes = [Border 1, CellBorder 0, CellSpacing 3, CellPadding 1] in
438	        Table $ HTable Nothing tableAttributes $ map (renderLine labelColor) sortedAbbrevs
439	
440	    -- | Render an abbreviation to a table row in the legend using graphviz' HTML notation.
441	    renderLine :: D.Color -> (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion) -> Row
442	    renderLine labelColor (abbrevName, recursiveExpansion) =
443	      let cellAttributes = [Align HLeft, VAlign HTop]
444	          font txt = D.Text [Font [Color labelColor] txt]
445	          name = LabelCell cellAttributes (font [Str $ T.pack $ render $ Sys.prettyLNTerm abbrevName])
446	          eq = LabelCell cellAttributes (D.Text [Str $ "="])
447	          expansion = render $ Sys.prettyLNTerm recursiveExpansion
448	          -- The expansions can get pretty big, i.e. span multiple lines. To handle linebreaks we replace them with HTML <br> tags.
449	          expansionBR = LabelCell cellAttributes (D.Text $ expansion `joinLinesWith` Newline [Align HLeft]) in
450	      Cells [name, eq, expansionBR]
451	
452	    -- | Replace newlines in `s` with the given separator.
453	    joinLinesWith :: String -> TextItem -> D.Text
454	    joinLinesWith s sep = intersperse sep $ map (Str . T.pack) $ lines s
455	
456	    -- | Do a topological sort of abbreviations to ensure that we print abbreviations in the order in which they are defined.
457	    -- We define a partial order where for two abbreviations A & B, it holds A < B if A appears in the recursive expansion of B.
458	    -- To extend the partial order to all abbreviations we do a topological sort on the graph where the nodes are abbreviations and edges are based on the partial order.
459	    topoSortAbbrevs :: [(Int, (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion))] -> [(AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion)]
460	    topoSortAbbrevs keyedElems =
461	      let edgeList = map (\(key1, node) ->
462	                            let outlist = findLegendEdges keyedElems node in
463	                            (node, key1, outlist)) keyedElems
464	          (graph, vf, _) = G.graphFromEdges edgeList
465	          vertices = G.topSort graph in
466	      map (\v -> fst3 $ vf v) vertices
467	
468	    -- | We create an edge A -> B between two abbreviations A & B if A appears in the recursive expansion of B.
469	    findLegendEdges :: [(Int, (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion))] -> (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion) -> [Int]
470	    findLegendEdges keyedElems (abbrevName1, _) =
471	      mapMaybe (\(key2, (_, recursiveExpansion2)) ->
472	                  if isProperSubterm abbrevName1 recursiveExpansion2
473	                  then Just key2
474	                  else Nothing) keyedElems

489	-- | Dot a graph in compact form (one record per rule).
490	dotGraphCompact :: DotOptions -> NodeColorMap -> Graph -> D.Dot ()
491	dotGraphCompact dotOptions colorMap graph =
492	    (`evalStateT` DotState M.empty M.empty M.empty M.empty) $
493	        (`runReaderT` (graph, colorMap, dotOptions)) $ do
494	
495	            -- Get the graph representation details
496	            let repr = get gRepr graph
497	                clusters = get grClusters repr
498	                edges = get grEdges repr
499	                nodes = get grNodes repr
500	                (lessEdges, restEdges) = mergeLessEdges edges
501	                abbreviate = get ((L..) goAbbreviate gOptions) graph
502	
503	            if null $ get grClusters repr then liftDot setDefaultAttributes else liftDot setDefaultAttributesIfCluster
504	
505	            -- Process the nodes, clusters, and edges
506	            mapM_ (\node -> dotNodeCompact node Nothing) nodes
507	            mapM_ dotCluster clusters
508	            mapM_ dotEdge restEdges
509	            mapM_ dotLessEdge lessEdges
510	            dotClustersEdges clusters

512	            -- Generate legend if abbreviate is set
513	            when abbreviate generateLegend

540	    r = floor (rf * 255) :: Int

564	-- | Compute proper colors for all less-edges.
565	-- Computing the colors requires all less-edges of the graph, so we cannot do it per edge.
566	-- This is why we split up the edges here and use two different functions to convert them to a dot.
567	mergeLessEdges :: [Edge] -> ([(NodeId, NodeId, String)], [Edge])
568	mergeLessEdges edges = (merged, rest)
569	  where
570	    rest = filter (not . isLessEdge) edges
571	    merged =
572	      let lessEdges = mapMaybe extractLessEdge edges in
573	      map getAllRToC $ eqClasses (\(LessAtom x y _) -> (x, y)) lessEdges
574	
575	    isLessEdge :: Edge -> Bool
576	    isLessEdge (LessEdge _) = True
577	    isLessEdge _ = False
578	
579	    extractLessEdge :: Edge -> Maybe LessAtom
580	    extractLessEdge (LessEdge e) = Just e
581	    extractLessEdge _ = Nothing
582	
583	    getAllRToC :: [LessAtom] -> (NodeId, NodeId, String)
584	    -- SAFETY: Output of eqClasses never contains the empty list.
585	    getAllRToC [] = error "empty list"
586	    getAllRToC xs@(x:_) =
587	      (get laSmaller x, get laLarger x,
588	        -- Sort order is reversed to put the "most important reason" first.
589	        allRtoColors (sortBy (comparing Data.Ord.Down) $ map (get laReason) xs))
590	
591	    allRtoColors :: [Reason] -> String
592	    allRtoColors reasons =
593	      let len = length reasons
594	          per = if len >1 then ";" ++ show ((1 :: Double)/fromIntegral len) else ""
595	          colors = map toColor reasons
596	          scaledColors = map (++per) colors
597	      in intercalate ":" scaledColors
598	
599	    toColor :: Reason -> String
600	    toColor r = case r of
601	         Adversary      -> "red"
602	         Formula        -> "black"
603	         Fresh          -> "blue3"
604	         InjectiveFacts -> "purple"
605	         NormalForm     -> "darkorange3"

9	-- This implements generating abbreviations for a given graph representation.
10	-- The abbreviations are computed from all LNTerm's in the graph and the method is based on cleandot.py 
11	-- All terms in the graph are weighted based on their multiplicity and length, highest weighted terms are abbreviated first.

41	-- | Type for the abbreviation itself, which is always a literal containing the string representation.
42	type AbbreviationTerm = LNTerm
43	-- | Type for the expanded abbreviation, which is the original term where subterms are replaced by other abbreviations where possible.
44	type AbbreviationExpansion = LNTerm
45	-- | Abbreviations are represented as a map from the original LNTerm 
46	-- to the abbreviated LNTerm and the original LNTerm where all existing
47	-- abbreviations have already been applied.
48	-- ==== __Example__:
49	--   [ Function1(a,b)              => Fun1, Function1(a,b)
50	--   , Function2(Function1(a,b),c) => Fun2, Function2(Fun1,c)
51	--   ]
52	type Abbreviations = M.Map LNTerm (AbbreviationTerm, AbbreviationExpansion)
53	
54	type Weight = Int
55	-- | Options to control the abbreviation generation.
56	data AbbreviationOptions = AbbreviationOptions 
57	  { aoAbbrevsSoftLimit   :: Int    -- ^ The soft limit of how many abbreviations to generate if the term weight is not greater or equal than 'aoAlwaysAbbrevWeight'.
58	  , aoAlwaysAbbrevWeight :: Weight -- ^ Terms that have weight greater or equal to this always generate an abbreviation even if the number of abbreviations is greater than 'aoAbbrevsSoftLimit'.
59	  , aoFirstIndex         :: Int    -- ^ The first index to use when generating abbreviations.
60	  , aoPrefixLength       :: Int    -- ^ The length of an abbreviation prefixes.
61	  }
62	  deriving( Eq, Ord )
63	
64	
65	-- | The default abbreviation options.
66	defaultAbbreviationOptions :: AbbreviationOptions
67	defaultAbbreviationOptions = AbbreviationOptions 
68	  { aoAbbrevsSoftLimit = 10
69	  , aoAlwaysAbbrevWeight = 30 -- a.d. TODO what is a good default value for this?
70	  , aoFirstIndex = 1
71	  , aoPrefixLength = 2
72	  }

74	-- | Lookup a single term in an abbreviations map and return the abbreviation name.
75	lookupAbbreviation :: Abbreviations -> LNTerm -> Maybe AbbreviationTerm
76	lookupAbbreviation abbrevs t = fst <$> M.lookup t abbrevs
77	
78	-- | Weigh each term based on its size and how often it appears in the graph.
79	judgeTerm :: M.Map LNTerm LNTerm -- ^ The existing set of abbreviations used to compute the screen size of the current term under consideration.
80	          -> LNTerm              -- ^ The current term to consider for abbreviating.
81	          -> (Int, [Int])        -- ^ The total number of occurrences and a list where each element is the number of occurrences in the existing set of abbreviations.
82	          -> Weight              -- ^ The resulting weight we give to that abbreviation.
83	judgeTerm abbrevs t (occs, legendOccs) 
84	  | termWeight < 10 = -1
85	  | relativeOccs <= 1 = -1
86	  | otherwise = relativeOccs * termWeight
87	  where
88	    -- | The termsize is the length of the term rendered as a string.
89	    termWeight :: Weight
90	    termWeight = 
91	      let replacedTerm = applyAbbreviationsTerm (\k -> M.lookup k abbrevs) t in
92	      length $ render $ prettyLNTerm replacedTerm
93	    
94	    -- | A "relative" occurrence is computed by comparing the total number of occurrences
95	    -- with the number of occurrences only in the legend.
96	    -- If we find the only occurrence of a term only in the legend then we will never abbreviate it.
97	    relativeOccs :: Int
98	    relativeOccs = 
99	      if occs == 1 && legendOccs == [1]
100	      then 0
101	      else occs
102	
103	-- | Find a suitable abbreviation prefix for the given term in upper-case.
104	-- For a literal we take the first 2 characters and for function symbols we either special-case the name or also take the first 2 characters.
105	-- If more fine-grained control is needed we can use viewTerm2 instead of viewTerm.
106	getTermPrefix :: AbbreviationOptions -> LNTerm -> String
107	getTermPrefix options t = map toUpper $ take (aoPrefixLength options) $ filter isAlpha go
108	  where
109	    go = case viewTerm t of
110	         Lit l                  -> show l
111	         FApp   (NoEq (s,_)) _  -> BC.unpack s
112	         -- a.d. TODO what is a good abbreviation for the "emap"?
113	         FApp   (C _) _         -> "EMP"
114	         FApp   List _          -> "LST"
115	         FApp   (AC o) _        -> show o
116	
117	
118	type PrefixMap = M.Map String Int
119	
120	-- | For a given term `t`, find a suitable abbreviation and update the prefix index map.
121	-- The prefixMap maps a prefix to the next possible index candidate.
122	abbreviateTerm :: AbbreviationOptions -> [String] -> PrefixMap -> LNTerm -> (PrefixMap, LNTerm)
123	abbreviateTerm options allNames prefixMap t = 
124	  let pref = getTermPrefix options t
125	      idxCandidate = getIndexCandidate pref in
126	    go pref idxCandidate
127	  where
128	    -- | Try out successive index candidates to create an abbreviation. 
129	    -- For each candidate we create the abbreviation and check if it is contained in the global map.
130	    -- If it already exists we try the next index candidate.
131	    go :: String -> Int -> (PrefixMap, LNTerm)
132	    go pref idxCandidate = 
133	      let nameCandidate = pref ++ show idxCandidate 
134	          idxCandidateNext = idxCandidate + 1 in
135	      if nameCandidate `elem` allNames 
136	      then go pref idxCandidateNext
137	      else (M.insert pref idxCandidateNext prefixMap, var nameCandidate)
138	
139	    -- | Check the prefix index map for a suitable index for the given prefix.
140	    getIndexCandidate :: String -> Int
141	    getIndexCandidate pref = fromMaybe (aoFirstIndex options) $ M.lookup pref prefixMap 
142	        
143	    -- | Create a variable from some prefix and index. 
144	    -- We append the index to the string directly instead of using the variable index since 
145	    -- the variable renderer inserts a point between name and index.
146	    var :: String -> LNTerm
147	    var name = 
148	      let  in
149	      lit $ Var $ LVar name LSortMsg 0
150	    
151	
152	-- | For a given graph representation, compute possible abbreviations that can be applied.
153	-- Abbreviations are always LNTerm variables with a differentiating number suffix.
154	--
155	-- actually what would lead to the best abbreviations is:
156	--   calculate terms + occurrences
157	--   calculate weights
158	--   take term X with maximum weight and make abbreviation
159	--   now for each subterm of X, decrement its occurrence (this can be reasonable fast if we keep the allTermOccs and just delete X, then go through all its subterms and decrement them)
160	--     this is good in a situation where 
161	--   for each other term that has X as a subterm, reduce replace X by its abbreviation when calculating the term size (this can be made fast by keeping around the list of abbreviations and applying it to each expression when calculating the term size)
162	--     this is good in a situation where you have a term X = F(Y) where Y has bigger weight than X and is abbreviated first. When Y is replaced by an abbreviation the weight of X drops a lot and probably does not need to be abbreviated.
163	--   repeat finding the next term with maximum weight
164	--   This algorithm is probably cubic in the number of terms, as finding all subterms is already quadratic and now we do this for each term.
165	    
166	computeAbbreviations :: GraphRepr -> AbbreviationOptions -> Abbreviations 
167	computeAbbreviations repr options = makeRecursive $ M.toList $ go allTermOccs M.empty M.empty
168	  where
169	    go :: M.Map LNTerm (Int, [Int]) -> PrefixMap -> M.Map LNTerm LNTerm -> M.Map LNTerm LNTerm
170	    go termOccs prefixMap abbrevs = 
171	      let weightedTerms = M.mapWithKey (judgeTerm abbrevs) termOccs
172	          candidateM = filterCandidateTerm $ M.toList weightedTerms
173	      in
174	        case candidateM of
175	          Nothing -> abbrevs
176	          Just (candidate, weight) -> 
177	            -- If the candidate weight is too low and we already have enough abbreviations, return the current abbreviations.
178	            if weight < aoAlwaysAbbrevWeight options && length abbrevs >= aoAbbrevsSoftLimit options
179	            then abbrevs
180	            else 
181	              -- make an abbreviation for this term
182	              let (newPrefixMap, abbrevName) = abbreviateTerm options allNames prefixMap candidate
183	                  -- Delete the candidate from the list of terms and decrement the occurrences of other terms.
184	                  newTermOccs = M.mapWithKey (\term (occs, legendOccs) -> (occs, countProperSubterms term candidate : legendOccs))
185	                                $ M.delete candidate termOccs
186	                  newAbbrevs = M.insert candidate abbrevName abbrevs
187	              in
188	                go newTermOccs newPrefixMap newAbbrevs
189	              
190	            
191	    -- | Collect all terms in our graph along with their number of occurrences
192	    allTermOccs :: M.Map LNTerm (Int, [Int])
193	    allTermOccs = 
194	      let terms = concatMap getNodeTerms (get grNodes repr) ++ concatMap (\c -> concatMap getNodeTerms $ get cNodes c) (get grClusters repr) in
195	      M.map (\occs -> (occs, [])) $ M.fromListWith (+) $ map (\t -> (t,1)) terms
196	
197	    -- | Collect all terms in a single graph node. 
198	    -- At the moment terms only appear in SystemNodes and UnsolvedActionNodes.
199	    getNodeTerms :: Node -> [LNTerm]
200	    getNodeTerms (Node _ (SystemNode ru)) =
201	      concatMap getFactTerms (get rPrems ru)
202	      ++ concatMap getFactTerms (get rActs ru)
203	      ++ concatMap getFactTerms (get rConcs ru) 
204	    getNodeTerms (Node _ (UnsolvedActionNode facts)) = concatMap getFactTerms facts
205	    getNodeTerms _ = []
206	
207	    -- | Collect all terms of a LNFact.
208	    -- We filter out pairs so that we do not abbreviate pairs of terms but only the inner terms.
209	    getFactTerms :: LNFact -> [LNTerm]
210	    getFactTerms fact = filter (not . isPair) $ concatMap getSubTerms $ factTerms fact 
211	
212	    -- | Subterms are the term itself along with ts of an FApp
213	    getSubTerms :: LNTerm -> [LNTerm]
214	    getSubTerms t@(viewTerm -> FApp _ ts) = t : ts
215	    getSubTerms t = [t]
216	
217	    -- | Collect all names that appear somewhere in the graph to avoid generating abbreviations with the same name as existing objects.
218	    --  quickest way to do this is to render the entire graph as a string and pull out all alphanumeric substrings.
219	    --  We convert all names to upper-case to compare in a case-insensitive way.
220	    allNames :: [String]
221	    allNames = 
222	      let rendered = T.pack $ show repr
223	          names = filter (not . null) $ map (T.unpack . T.toUpper) $ T.split (not . isAlphaNum) rendered
224	      in
225	        sort $ nub names
226	
227	    -- | For a number of weighted terms, take the first aoMaxAbbrevs terms sorted by decreasing weight.
228	    filterCandidateTerm :: [(LNTerm, Weight)] -> Maybe (LNTerm, Weight)
229	    filterCandidateTerm weightedTerms = 
230	      let relevantWeights = sortOn (Data.Ord.Down . snd) $ filter (\(_, weight) -> weight > 0) weightedTerms in
231	      listToMaybe relevantWeights
232	
233	    -- | For a given list of terms, find suitable abbreviations and return a mapping from the original to the abbreviated terms. 
234	    -- The prefix index map is initially empty and accumulates the next possible index candidate for each prefix while we create abbreviations.
235	    abbreviateTerms :: [LNTerm] -> [(LNTerm, LNTerm)]
236	    abbreviateTerms ts =
237	      let (_, abbrevNames) = mapAccumL (abbreviateTerm options allNames) M.empty ts in
238	      zip ts abbrevNames
239	
240	    -- | For a given list of (expansion, abbrevName) pairs, compute a recursiveExpansion that is the original expansion term where any subterm which is also an expansion is replaced by the respective abbrevName. 
241	    -- Going through all expansions once is enough because abbrevNames do not contain any subterms themselves, so we do not introduce any new possibilities for recursive occurrences. 
242	    --
243	    -- ==== __Example__:
244	    -- [(A(B, C), H1), (B, H2)] => [(A(H2, C), H1), (B, H2)]
245	    makeRecursive :: [(LNTerm, LNTerm)] -> Abbreviations
246	    makeRecursive abbrevs = 
247	      let replacementMap = M.fromList abbrevs 
248	          replacement t = fromMaybe t (M.lookup t replacementMap)
249	          recursiveExpansions = map (replaceProperSubterm replacement . fst) abbrevs
250	          recursiveAbbrevs = zipWith (\(expansion, abbrevName) recursiveExpansion -> 
251	                                        (expansion, (abbrevName, recursiveExpansion))) 
252	                                      abbrevs recursiveExpansions
253	      in
254	        M.fromList recursiveAbbrevs
255	
256	-- | Go through an LNTerm and replace all possible subterms with their abbreviation top-down.
257	applyAbbreviationsTerm :: (LNTerm -> Maybe AbbreviationTerm) -> LNTerm -> LNTerm
258	applyAbbreviationsTerm lookupTerm term = 
259	  let replaceWithAbbrev t = fromMaybe t $ lookupTerm t
260	      replacedTerm = replaceSubterm replaceWithAbbrev term
261	  in
262	    replacedTerm
263	
264	-- | Go through an LNFact and apply the abbreviations to all contained LNTerm's.
265	applyAbbreviationsFact :: (LNTerm -> Maybe AbbreviationTerm) -> LNFact -> LNFact
266	applyAbbreviationsFact lookupTerm fact = 
267	  let terms = factTerms fact
268	      replacedTerms = map (applyAbbreviationsTerm lookupTerm) terms
269	  in 
270	    fact { factTerms = replacedTerms }
271

9	--
10	-- Representation of a graph as a collection of nodes, edges and clusters that can be used for rendering a System.
11	module Theory.Constraint.System.Graph.GraphRepr (
12	      GraphRepr(..)
13	    , grNodes
14	    , grClusters
15	    , grEdges
16	    , Node(..)
17	    , nNodeType
18	    , nNodeId
19	    , NodeType(..)
20	    , Edge(..)
21	    , Cluster(..)
22	    , cName
23	    , cNodes
24	    , cEdges
25	    , toEdgeList
26	    , extractRole
27	    , getNodeRole
28	    , getNodeName
29	    , groupNodesByRole
30	    , addClusterByRole
31	    , addIntelligentClusterUsingSimilarNames
32	    , extractBaseName
33	    , getRuleNameByNode
34	  ) where
35	
36	import           Extension.Data.Label
37	import qualified Theory.Constraint.System as Sys
38	import qualified Theory.Model             as M
39	import qualified Theory                   as Th
40	import qualified Data.Map                 as Map
41	
42	import qualified Data.Set                 as S
43	
44	import Data.Char (isDigit)
45	import Data.List.Split (splitOn)
46	import Data.List (find, intercalate)
47	import Data.Maybe
48	
49	-- | All nodes are identified by their NodeId.
50	-- Then we have different types of nodes depending on what data of the System they use.
51	data Node = Node {
52	    _nNodeId    :: M.NodeId,
53	    _nNodeType  :: NodeType
54	  }
55	  deriving( Eq, Ord, Show )
56	
57	-- | Different types of graph nodes.
58	data NodeType =
59	    SystemNode Th.RuleACInst                    -- ^ Nodes from rule instances
60	  | UnsolvedActionNode [Th.LNFact]             -- ^ Nodes from unsolved adversary actions.
61	  | LastActionAtom                             -- ^ Nodes that are only used for induction.
62	  | MissingNode (Either Th.ConcIdx Th.PremIdx) -- ^ Nodes referenced by edges which don't exist elsewhere.
63	  deriving( Eq, Ord, Show )
64	
65	
66	-- | Different types of graph edges.
67	data Edge =
68	    SystemEdge (Sys.NodeConc, Sys.NodePrem)    -- ^ Edges that transport facts from premises to conclusions between rules.
69	  | LessEdge Th.LessAtom                       -- ^ Edges that represent a temporal-before relationship.
70	  | UnsolvedChain (Sys.NodeConc, Sys.NodePrem) -- ^ Edges that are part of an unsolved chain between premises and conclusions.
71	  deriving( Eq, Ord, Show )
72	
73	-- | A cluster contains nodes, edges, and a name, which is the common prefix of the contained nodes.
74	data Cluster = Cluster {
75	    _cName  :: String
76	  , _cNodes :: [Node]
77	  , _cEdges :: [Edge]
78	  }
79	  deriving( Eq, Ord, Show )
80	
81	-- | A graph consists of nodes, edges and clusters which are only one level deep to represent a collection of derivation rules with the same prefix.
82	data GraphRepr = GraphRepr {
83	    _grClusters :: [Cluster]
84	  , _grNodes    :: [Node]
85	  , _grEdges    :: [Edge]
86	  }
87	  deriving ( Eq, Ord, Show )
88	
89	$(mkLabels [''GraphRepr, ''Node, ''Cluster])
90	
91	-- | Conversion function to a list of edges as used by Data.Graph.
92	toEdgeList :: GraphRepr -> [(Node, M.NodeId, [M.NodeId])]
93	toEdgeList repr =
94	  let allNodes = get grNodes repr ++ concatMap (get cNodes) (get grClusters repr)
95	      allEdges = get grEdges repr ++ concatMap (get cEdges) (get grClusters repr) in
96	  map (\node -> (node, get nNodeId node, findSinkIndices allEdges node)) allNodes
97	  where
98	    -- | For each node, find all connected nodes using allEdges and return their NodeId's.
99	    findSinkIndices :: [Edge] -> Node -> [M.NodeId]
100	    findSinkIndices allEdges node =
101	      let srcId = get nNodeId node in
102	      mapMaybe (findEdgeTarget srcId) allEdges
103	
104	    -- | For a given source node id and an edge, check if the edge belongs to the node and return the target node id.
105	    findEdgeTarget :: M.NodeId -> Edge -> Maybe M.NodeId
106	    findEdgeTarget srcId (SystemEdge ((srcId', _), (tgtId, _)))    | srcId == srcId' = Just tgtId
107	    findEdgeTarget srcId (LessEdge (Th.LessAtom srcId' tgtId _))   | srcId == srcId' = Just tgtId
108	    findEdgeTarget srcId (UnsolvedChain ((srcId', _), (tgtId, _))) | srcId == srcId' = Just tgtId
109	    findEdgeTarget _     _                                                           = Nothing
110	
111	
112	----------------------------------------------------
113	-- Clusturing
114	----------------------------------------------------
115	
116	-- Function to add clusters to a GraphRepr
117	addCluster :: GraphRepr -> Map.Map String [Node] -> String -> GraphRepr
118	addCluster repr nodesByGroup nameSuffix =
119	    let edges = get grEdges repr
120	        createSubClusters name nodes =
121	            let connectedComponents = findConnectedComponents nodes (filterEdgesForCluster nodes edges)
122	            in zipWith (\i component -> createCluster (name ++ nameSuffix ++ show (i :: Integer)) component (filterEdgesForCluster component edges)) [1..] connectedComponents
123	        subClusters = concatMap (uncurry createSubClusters) (Map.toList nodesByGroup)
124	        clusterEdges = concatMap (get cEdges) subClusters
125	        clusteredNodes = concatMap (get cNodes) subClusters
126	        remainingEdges = filter (`notElem` clusterEdges) edges
127	        remainingNodes = filter (`notElem` clusteredNodes) (get grNodes repr)
128	    in set grClusters subClusters $
129	       set grEdges remainingEdges $
130	       set grNodes remainingNodes repr
131	
132	----------------------------------------------------
133	-- Clusturing by role name
134	----------------------------------------------------
135	
136	extractRole :: Th.RuleACInst -> Maybe String
137	extractRole ru = Th.role (Th.ruleAttributes ru)
138	
139	groupNodesByRole :: [Node] -> Map.Map String [Node]
140	groupNodesByRole = foldr groupByRole Map.empty
141	  where
142	    groupByRole node acc = case getNodeRole node of
143	      Just role     -> Map.insertWith (++) role [node] acc
144	      Nothing        -> acc
145	
146	
147	getNodeName :: Node -> String
148	getNodeName node = "node" ++ show (get nNodeId node)
149	
150	getNodeRole :: Node -> Maybe String
151	getNodeRole node = case get nNodeType node of
152	  SystemNode ru -> extractRole ru
153	  _             -> Nothing
154	
155	
156	-- Function to create a cluster from an role's nodes and relevant edges
157	createCluster :: String -> [Node] -> [Edge] -> Cluster
158	createCluster = Cluster
159	
160	-- Filters edges to include only those relevant for the nodes of a cluster
161	filterEdgesForCluster :: [Node] -> [Edge] -> [Edge]
162	filterEdgesForCluster nodes edges =
163	    let nodeIds = S.fromList (map (get nNodeId) nodes)
164	    in filter (\edge -> case edge of
165	                            SystemEdge ((srcNode, _), (tgtNode, _)) -> srcNode `S.member` nodeIds && tgtNode `S.member` nodeIds
166	                            UnsolvedChain ((srcNode, _), (tgtNode, _)) -> srcNode `S.member` nodeIds && tgtNode `S.member` nodeIds
167	                            LessEdge (Th.LessAtom srcNode tgtNode _) -> srcNode `S.member` nodeIds && tgtNode `S.member` nodeIds) edges
168	
169	-- Function to find the connected components within a cluster
170	findConnectedComponents :: [Node] -> [Edge] -> [[Node]]
171	findConnectedComponents nodes edges = go nodes []
172	  where
173	    -- Recursive function to find all nodes connected from a given node
174	    expandCluster :: Node -> S.Set Th.NodeId -> [Node] -> [Edge] -> S.Set Th.NodeId
175	    expandCluster node visited allNodes allEdges =
176	      let nodeId = get nNodeId node
177	          connectedNodes = [ tgt | SystemEdge ((src, _), (tgt, _)) <- allEdges, src == nodeId, tgt `S.notMember` visited ] ++
178	                           [ src | SystemEdge ((src, _), (tgt, _)) <- allEdges, tgt == nodeId, src `S.notMember` visited ]
179	          newVisited = S.insert nodeId visited
180	      in foldr (\nid acc -> if nid `S.member` visited then acc else expandCluster (findNodeById nid allNodes) newVisited allNodes allEdges `S.union` acc) (S.singleton nodeId) connectedNodes
181	
182	    findNodeById :: Th.NodeId -> [Node] -> Node
183	    findNodeById nodeId allNodes = head $ filter (\n -> get nNodeId n == nodeId) allNodes
184	
185	    -- Main function to find all connected components
186	    go :: [Node] -> [[Node]] -> [[Node]]
187	    go [] components = components
188	    go (n:ns) components =
189	      let componentIds = S.toList $ expandCluster n S.empty (n:ns) edges
190	          component = filter (\node -> get nNodeId node `elem` componentIds) (n:ns)
191	          remainingNodes = filter (`notElem` component) ns
192	      in go remainingNodes (component : components)
193	
194	
195	-- Function to add sub-clusters by role
196	addClusterByRole :: GraphRepr -> GraphRepr
197	addClusterByRole repr =
198	    let nodesByRole = groupNodesByRole (get grNodes repr)
199	    in addCluster repr nodesByRole "_Session_"
200	
201	
202	
203	----------------------------------------------------
204	-- Clustering based on the name of the rules.
205	----------------------------------------------------
206	
207	-- Function to get the rule name from a node
208	getRuleNameByNode :: Node -> Maybe String
209	getRuleNameByNode node =
210	    case _nNodeType node of
211	        SystemNode ru -> case Th.ruleName ru of
212	                           Th.ProtoInfo _ -> Just (Th.showRuleCaseName ru)
213	                           _ -> Nothing
214	        _ -> Nothing
215	
216	-- Function to extract the base name based on underscores
217	extractBaseName :: String -> Maybe String
218	extractBaseName name =
219	    let parts = splitOn "_" name
220	        lastPart = last parts
221	        isNumber = all isDigit lastPart
222	        baseName = if isNumber && length parts > 1
223	                   then Just (intercalate "_" (init parts))
224	                   else Nothing
225	    in baseName
226	
227	-- Function to group nodes by similar rule names
228	groupBySimilarName :: [Node] -> Map.Map String [Node]
229	groupBySimilarName nodes =
230	    let result = foldr (\node acc ->
231	                    case getRuleNameByNode node >>= extractBaseName of
232	                        Just baseName -> Map.insertWith (++) baseName [node] acc
233	                        Nothing       -> acc
234	                ) Map.empty nodes
235	    in result
236	
237	-- Function to add intelligent clusters using similar rule names
238	addIntelligentClusterUsingSimilarNames :: GraphRepr -> GraphRepr
239	addIntelligentClusterUsingSimilarNames repr =
240	    let nodesBySimilarName = groupBySimilarName (get grNodes repr)
241	    in addCluster repr nodesBySimilarName "_Session_"

3	-- |

5	-- License     : GPL v3 (see LICENSE)
6	--
7	-- Portability : GHC only
8	--
9	-- System simplification functions that were originally contained in the Dot module 
10	-- but which we now do on a System when generating an abstract graph.
11	module Theory.Constraint.System.Graph.Simplification (
12	      simplifySystem
13	    , compressSystem
14	  )
15	  where
16	
17	import qualified Data.Map                 as M
18	import           Data.Maybe
19	import qualified Data.Set                 as S
20	import           Data.List                (foldl')
21	import           Control.Basics
22	import           Extension.Data.Label
23	-- import           Theory.Constraint.System
24	import           Theory                   
25	import qualified Data.DAG.Simple          as Dag
26	import           Data.Monoid              (Any(..))
27	
28	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
29	-- Compressed versions of a sequent (originally from Dot module)
30	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
31	
32	-- | Drop 'Less' atoms entailed by the edges of the 'System'.
33	dropEntailedOrdConstraints :: System -> System
34	dropEntailedOrdConstraints se =
35	    modify sLessAtoms (S.filter (not . entailed)) se
36	  where
37	    edges               = rawEdgeRel se
38	    entailed (LessAtom from to _) = to `S.member` Dag.reachableSet [from] edges
39	
40	-- | Unsound compression of the sequent that drops fully connected learns and
41	-- knows nodes.
42	compressSystem :: System -> System
43	compressSystem se0 =
44	    foldl' (flip tryHideNodeId) se (frees (get sLessAtoms se, get sNodes se))
45	  where
46	    se = dropEntailedOrdConstraints se0
47	
48	-- | Simplify the system up to the sent level, 
49	-- | for level 3, we will simplify the lesses of system by transitive reduction
50	-- | for level 2, we will apply the transitive reduction but keep the 
51	-- | formula constraint 
52	-- | for level 1, there's no transitive reduction applied
53	simplifySystem :: Int -> System -> System
54	simplifySystem i sys
55	    | i==2 = transitiveReduction sys False
56	    | i==3 = transitiveReduction sys True
57	    | otherwise = sys
58	
59	-- | Simplify the system by transitive reduction (constraint of formula won't  
60	-- | be applied if totalRed is False) but not for a system which has a graph cyclic
61	transitiveReduction :: System -> Bool -> System
62	transitiveReduction sys totalRed=
63	    if Dag.cyclic oldLesses
64	        then sys
65	        else   modify sLessAtoms
66	            ( S.intersection ( S.fromList newLesses) ) sys
67	    where
68	        oldLessesWithR = S.toList $ get sLessAtoms sys
69	        oldLesses = rawLessRel sys
70	        newLesses = if totalRed
71	            then [ la | la@(LessAtom x y _) <- oldLessesWithR,
72	                            (x,y) `elem` Dag.transRed oldLesses ]
73	            else [ la | la@(LessAtom x y z) <- oldLessesWithR,
74	                            (x,y) `elem` Dag.transRed oldLesses || z == Formula || z == Adversary ]
75	
76	
77	-- | @hideTransferNode v se@ hides node @v@ in sequent @se@ if it is a
78	-- transfer node; i.e., a node annotated with a rule that is one of the
79	-- special intruder rules or a rule with with at most one premise and
80	-- at most one conclusion and both premises and conclusions have incoming
81	-- respectively outgoing edges.
82	--
83	-- The compression is chosen such that unly uninteresting nodes are that have
84	-- no open goal are suppressed.
85	tryHideNodeId :: NodeId -> System -> System
86	tryHideNodeId v se = fromMaybe se $ do
87	    guard $  (lvarSort v == LSortNode)
88	          && notOccursIn unsolvedChains
89	          && notOccursIn (get sFormulas)
90	    maybe hideAction hideRule (M.lookup v $ get sNodes se)
91	  where
92	    selectPart :: (System :-> S.Set a) -> (a -> Bool) -> [a]
93	    selectPart l p = filter p $ S.toList $ get l se
94	
95	    notOccursIn :: HasFrees a => (System -> a) -> Bool
96	    notOccursIn proj = not $ getAny $ foldFrees (Any . (v ==)) $ proj se
97	
98	    -- hide KU-actions deducing pairs, inverses, and simple terms
99	    hideAction = do
100	        guard $  not (null kuActions)
101	              && all eligibleTerm kuActions
102	              && all (\(LessAtom i j _) -> i /= j) lNews
103	              && notOccursIn standardActionAtoms
104	              && notOccursIn (get sLastAtom)
105	              && notOccursIn (get sEdges)
106	
107	        return $ modify sLessAtoms ( (`S.union` S.fromList lNews)
108	                                   . (`S.difference` S.fromList lIns)
109	                                   . (`S.difference` S.fromList lOuts)
110	                                   )
111	               $ modify sGoals (\m -> foldl' removeAction m kuActions)
112	               $ se
113	      where
114	        kuActions            = [ x | x@(i,_,_) <- kuActionAtoms se, i == v ]
115	        eligibleTerm (_,_,m) =
116	            isPair m || isInverse m || sortOfLNTerm m == LSortPub || sortOfLNTerm m == LSortNat
117	
118	        removeAction m (i, fa, _) = M.delete (ActionG i fa) m
119	
120	        lIns  = selectPart sLessAtoms ((v ==) . get laLarger)
121	        lOuts = selectPart sLessAtoms ((v ==) . get laSmaller)
122	        lNews = [ LessAtom i j r | (LessAtom i _ _) <- lIns, (LessAtom _ j r) <- lOuts ]
123	
124	    -- hide a rule, if it is not "too complicated"
125	    hideRule :: RuleACInst -> Maybe System
126	    hideRule ru = do
127	        guard $  eligibleRule
128	              && ( length eIns  == length (get rPrems ru) )
129	              && ( length eOuts == length (get rConcs ru) )
130	              && ( all (not . selfEdge) eNews             )
131	              && notOccursIn (get sLastAtom)
132	              && notOccursIn (get sLessAtoms)
133	              && notOccursIn (unsolvedActionAtoms)
134	
135	        return $ modify sEdges ( (`S.union` S.fromList eNews)
136	                               . (`S.difference` S.fromList eIns)
137	                               . (`S.difference` S.fromList eOuts)
138	                               )
139	               $ modify sNodes (M.delete v)
140	               $ se
141	      where
142	        eIns  = selectPart sEdges ((v ==) . nodePremNode . eTgt)
143	        eOuts = selectPart sEdges ((v ==) . nodeConcNode . eSrc)
144	        eNews = [ Edge cIn pOut | Edge cIn _ <- eIns, Edge _ pOut <- eOuts ]
145	
146	        selfEdge (Edge cIn pOut) = nodeConcNode cIn == nodePremNode pOut
147	
148	        eligibleRule =
149	             any ($ ru) [isISendRule, isIRecvRule, isCoerceRule, isFreshRule]
150	          || ( null (get rActs ru) &&
151	               all (\l -> length (get l ru) <= 1) [rPrems, rConcs]
152	             )

# Similarity audit — graphdot cluster (clean room vs GPL Haskell)

Auditor role: SIMILARITY AUDITOR (may read both sides). Discipline:
abstraction–filtration–comparison. A match is a VIOLATION only if it is expression
that could not plausibly come from observing program OUTPUT: mirrored internal helper
decomposition where alternatives exist, matching internal (non-API, non-output) names,
comments echoing Haskell comments, algorithmic expression matching where the observed
behavior admits materially different implementations, or any content present in the
Haskell source but in NO observable output.

## Sides compared

CLEAN (reimplementation):
- `workspace/graph-clean/src/model.rs` — syntactic DOT graph model
- `workspace/graph-clean/src/dot.rs` — byte-exact DOT serializer
- `workspace/graph-clean/src/term.rs` — small term model for legend expansions
- `workspace/graph-clean/src/abbrev.rs` — prefix derivation, numbering, legend HTML
- `workspace/graph-clean/src/lib.rs` — module glue

HASKELL (originals, GPL):
- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Dot.hs`
- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/Abbreviation.hs`
- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/GraphRepr.hs`
- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/Simplification.hs`
- (secondary dirty-room Rust ports: `crates/tamarin-server/src/graph/{repr,abbreviation,simplify,options}.rs`,
  `handlers/dot.rs` — translations of the Haskell; used only to understand the mapping)

## Output-observability check (evidence the compatibility content is genuinely observed)

Grepped the captured DOT payloads at `oracle/captured_responses`
(→ `tamarin-rs/scripts/.web_hs_cache`, 81 payloads). Confirmed present in OUTPUT:
- compact header incl. `packmode="cluster"` (8 payloads); simple header `nodesep="0.3"`.
- legend `<TABLE … CELLPADDING="1">` (73 payloads); `role=` on record nodes (79 payloads).
- abbrev names as PREFIX+counter: `AE1 AM1 … FS… EX…`; the exact 65-space hang indent
  before continuation `<TR>`; HTML escaping `&/</>`; exp rendering `'g'^…`.
- `FS` (from `F_status`) and `AM` (from `AMF_UE_NGAP_ID`) both appear as OUTPUT names.

Consequence: every string constant and every naming/rendering behavior the clean side
reproduces is present in observed output, so it is compatibility content, not expression
copied from source.

Grepped the clean side for distinctive Haskell internal identifiers
(`judgeTerm`, `getTermPrefix`, `abbreviateTerm`, `makeRecursive`, `prefixMap`, `allNames`,
`relativeOccs`, `termWeight`, `topoSort`, `roleColor`, `simpleHash`, `generateValue`,
`renderBalanced`, `scaleIndent`, `dotNodeCompact`, `mergeLessEdges`, `tryHideNode`,
`compressSystem`, `transitiveReduction`, …): NONE present.

## VIOLATIONS

None. No finding survives filtration as expression that could not have come from
observation. No redo instructions are issued.

## Per-module analysis

### model.rs vs GraphRepr.hs — CLEARED (different abstraction level)
The clean model is a *syntactic* DOT model: `Header{Simple,Compact}`,
`Stmt{Node,Edge,Cluster,RankBlock}`, `NodeKind{Record,Ellipse,Plain}`,
`Record{columns,fillcolor,fontcolor,role}`, `Cell{port,text}`, `Ellipse`, `EndPoint`,
`Cluster{label,color,body}`, `RankBlock{rank,body}`. The Haskell `GraphRepr` is a
*semantic* model: `Node{nNodeId,nNodeType}`, `NodeType{SystemNode ru, UnsolvedActionNode,
LastActionAtom, MissingNode}`, `Edge{SystemEdge,LessEdge,UnsolvedChain}`,
`Cluster{cName,cNodes,cEdges}`. The two carry different data (records/ellipses/attrs vs
rule instances / premise-conclusion indices). No shared internal names, no mirrored
decomposition. Fields present are dictated by what the DOT output contains. Not a violation.

- `Role::UNDEFINED = "Undefined"` (model.rs:29) mirrors Haskell's
  `fromMaybe "Undefined" (getNodeRole node)` (Dot.hs:243) only because `role="Undefined"`
  is emitted into the DOT output (verified in 79 payloads). Sentinel derived from output.
- `infer_header` = "compact iff any node has a non-Undefined role" (model.rs:56-64).
  Haskell's actual trigger is `null grClusters` (Dot.hs:503). These are different internal
  predicates that happen to coincide behaviorally (clusters are built only from role-bearing
  nodes). The clean side chose a *different* expression of the same observable correlation.
  Not a violation.

### dot.rs vs Dot.hs — CLEARED (output strings + independent structure)
- `header_lines` Simple/Compact attribute lists (dot.rs:38-69) are identical in content
  and order to `setDefaultAttributes` / `setDefaultAttributesIfCluster` (Dot.hs:130-161).
  These are exact bytes emitted to output (verified) and their order is output-determined.
  Compatibility content. Critically, the Haskell per-attribute explanatory comments
  ("Slight increase to space out the graph", "Combine parallel edges", etc.) are NOT
  reproduced. Not a violation.
- Record/edge/endpoint/cluster/rankblock serialization (dot.rs:80-175) is plain Rust
  string formatting. Haskell builds via a monadic `Text.Dot` DSL (`D.record`, `D.node`,
  `D.edge`, StateT/ReaderT). Entirely different implementation strategy; the emitted bytes
  match because bytes are the observed contract. The `render_block` "blank line before `}`"
  rule (dot.rs:26-36) is reverse-engineered from output formatting. Not a violation.
- `subgraph "cluster_…"` prefix (dot.rs:165) is DOT output syntax present in payloads.

### term.rs vs Term.LTerm view (used in Abbreviation.hs) — CLEARED (renderings are output)
- `Term{Fresh,Pub,Msg,Const,Pair,Exp,Ac,App}` and its renderings `~n / $n / n / 'n' /
  <a, b, c> / base^e / (a op b) / f(a,b)` (term.rs:144-165) all reproduce surface syntax
  visible in node labels and legend expansions (verified `'g'^…`). The Ac/Exp-vs-App split
  is forced by observed output (AC operators and exp render infix; ordinary functions render
  prefix). Not a violation.
- `Ac` operator table `mult→* , union→++ , xor→⊕` and `root_symbol_name` mapping
  `mult/union/xor/exp/pair` (term.rs:57-83) use lowercase logical names, whereas Haskell
  derives the prefix from `show o` = `Mult/Union/Xor` and `BC.unpack s` = `exp`
  (Abbreviation.hs:109-116). Different source expression; identical observable prefixes
  (MU/UN/XO/EX seen in output). Not a violation.
- `for_each_subterm` is fully recursive pre-order (term.rs:104-118); Haskell `getSubTerms`
  is one level only (`t : ts`, Abbreviation.hs:213-215). Divergent, not mirrored.

### abbrev.rs vs Abbreviation.hs — CLEARED (closest calls documented below)
- Legend HTML (`TABLE_OPEN`, `legend_row`, `legend_html`, `escape_expansion`,
  abbrev.rs:19-151) reproduces exact output bytes: table/cell tags, `<FONT COLOR="#000000">`,
  the `=` cell, `&/</>` escaping, and the 65-space hang indent equal to
  `len(TABLE_OPEN)`. All verified in captured payloads; the indent was measured from output,
  not taken from the Haskell (whose layout comes from the `Text.Dot` pretty-printer, no such
  constant in source). Compatibility content. Not a violation.
- `Abbreviator` numbering: per-prefix `1,2,3,…` with dedup by full rendering
  (abbrev.rs:63-75). Names ARE the output (PREFIX+counter, verified). The clean side does
  NOT reproduce Haskell's collision-avoidance against `allNames`
  (Abbreviation.hs:135-137, 220-225), soft-limit / always-abbrev-weight
  (Abbreviation.hs:178), weight formula `judgeTerm` (Abbreviation.hs:83-101), or the
  legend `topoSortAbbrevs` ordering (Dot.hs:456-474) — all source-only machinery is absent.
  Not a violation.
- `select` (abbrev.rs:158-176) is an explicitly-labeled best-effort heuristic (count
  occurrences, size threshold, sort ascending by size). It is materially different from the
  Haskell greedy max-weight algorithm with subterm-occurrence decrementing
  (`computeAbbreviations`, Abbreviation.hs:166-188), and even sorts in the opposite
  direction. Not a violation.

#### Closest call (considered, CLEARED): prefix derivation
- Clean `prefix_for_symbol` (abbrev.rs:33-39):
  `chars.filter(is_ascii_alphabetic).take(2).flat_map(to_uppercase)`.
- Haskell `getTermPrefix` (Abbreviation.hs:107):
  `map toUpper . take (aoPrefixLength) . filter isAlpha`.
- The pipeline is the same three operations in the same order (filter-alpha → take-2 →
  uppercase). FILTRATION: the observed output forces exactly this semantics and even pins
  the operation ORDER. `F_status → FS` (present in output) can only arise from
  filter-then-take (take-then-filter would yield `F`); `h2 → H`, `KDF → KD`,
  `AMF_UE_NGAP_ID → AM`, `senc → SE` fix "first two alphabetic characters, uppercased,
  non-letters skipped, length 2." There is no materially different implementation of that
  forced behavior. Per protocol this is "identical behavior at the idea level" / "structure
  forced by the output format," not expression. CLEARED — no redo. (If a future reviewer
  wants extra distance anyway, the behavioral redo would be: derive the two-letter uppercase
  tag by scanning characters and collecting letters directly, rather than as a
  filter/take/map chain — but this is not required, as the current code is output-forced.)

### Simplification.hs / GraphRepr.hs clustering — NO CLEAN COUNTERPART
The clean cluster has no analog to `compressSystem`, `dropEntailedOrdConstraints`,
`transitiveReduction`, `tryHideNodeId` (Simplification.hs) or `addCluster`,
`groupNodesByRole`, `findConnectedComponents`, `extractBaseName`, `roleColor`/`simpleHash`
color hashing (GraphRepr.hs / Dot.hs:525-544). The clean side takes cluster label and color
as pre-computed inputs (model.rs:202-210). The one piece of source-only, non-observable
expression that would be a red flag if copied — the `simpleHash`/`generateValue`/`roleColor`
constants (31, 7, HSV 0.75/0.85, alpha 0.3) — is entirely ABSENT from the clean side.
Strong positive signal for non-access.

## Conclusion
The clean-room modules are an independent reimplementation. Every similarity to the Haskell
traces to observable OUTPUT (exact DOT/HTML strings, abbrev names, surface-syntax renderings)
or to behavior that the output forces. No mirrored internal decomposition, no shared internal
names, no echoed comments, and none of the source-only machinery (weight/greedy abbreviation,
collision avoidance, topo-sort ordering, color hashing, system simplification/clustering)
appears on the clean side. VERDICT: PASS. No redo instructions.
